docs.title: Risk RegisterCloud Security Risk Register
docs.text:
CSPM — Risk Register Business impact, likelihood, financial exposure, and remediation status for all tracked cloud security risks. 2Critical 0High 0Medium 0Low Overview What is a risk register · how to read it · audience guide ▾ What is a risk register? A structured record of identified security risks — what could go wrong, how likely it is, what the business impact would be, and what we are doing about it. It is the single source of truth for cloud security risk posture. For stakeholders The Stakeholder Summary section shows risks in business language — what is at risk, the financial exposure, and current status. No technical knowledge required. This is the view for leadership and business owners. For technical teams The Technical Detail section contains controls, compliance mapping, remediation steps, detection source, and residual risk per finding. This is the working view for Cloud Security, Platform, and IAM teams. How to update Edit this file directly on GitHub at docs/cspm/risk-register/index.md and commit to main. Changes are live within 60 seconds. To add a new risk, copy an existing risk block and update the fields. Stakeholder summary Business view · financial exposure · disruption impact · status 2 Critical 1 Open 1 In Progress ▾

This view is for business and executive audiences. It focuses on what is at risk, the business consequence, and current remediation status.

IAM-001 Overprivileged IAM Roles 🔴 Critical Open CategoryIAM EnvironmentAWS Production Financial exposureHigh > $500K Target date2026-06-15 OwnerCloud Platform Team ⚠ Business disruption Multiple IAM roles have administrator-level permissions without business justification. A single compromised credential could result in full environment takeover, data exfiltration, or ransomware deployment — affecting all cloud-hosted production workloads. Financial exposure includes breach notification, regulatory fines (GDPR/CCPA), incident response, and business disruption exceeding $500K. NET-001 Publicly Exposed Security Groups 🔴 Critical In Progress CategoryNetwork Security EnvironmentAWS Production Financial exposureHigh > $500K Target date2026-05-30 OwnerNetwork Security Team ⚠ Business disruption Security groups allow unrestricted inbound access from 0.0.0.0/0. Internet-facing workloads are directly exposed to scanning, brute force, and exploitation. For production EC2 instances this creates risk of service disruption, data exfiltration, and regulatory non-compliance. Financial exposure includes incident response, downtime, and potential fines exceeding $500K. Technical detail Controls · compliance mapping · remediation · residual risk ▾ IAM-001 Overprivileged IAM Roles 🔴 Critical ▾ Risk IDIAM-001 (CSN: IAM-IDC-001) CategoryIAM Cloud environmentAWS Production Affected assetsIAM Roles, EC2, Lambda Data classificationHighly Critical LikelihoodHigh (4/5) ImpactHigh (4/5) Risk score4/5 — Critical Detection sourceWiz Compliance mappingCIS 1.22, NIST AC-6 Existing controlsMFA enabled, CloudTrail logging Residual riskMedium Risk treatmentMitigate Exception approvedNo Target date2026-06-15 Supporting teamIAM Team ✓ Recommended remediation Implement least privilege access and remove wildcard permissions. Review all cross-account roles. Enforce permission boundaries on all IAM roles. Audit roles not used in 90 days and remove or disable. NET-001 Publicly Exposed Security Groups 🔴 Critical ▾ Risk IDNET-001 CategoryNetwork Security Cloud environmentAWS Production Affected assetsEC2 Security Groups Data classificationConfidential LikelihoodHigh (4/5) ImpactHigh (4/5) Risk score4/5 — Critical Detection sourceWiz Compliance mappingCIS 4.1, NIST SC-7 Existing controlsAWS Shield, WAF Residual riskLow Risk treatmentMitigate Exception approvedNo Target date2026-05-30 Supporting teamCloud Engineering ✓ Recommended remediation Restrict inbound traffic to known CIDR ranges and remove 0.0.0.0/0 rules. Implement network segmentation. Prioritise internet-facing assets. Enforce security group policies via AWS Config rules to prevent re-introduction. Guide Scoring methodology · financial exposure bands · severity levels ▾ Likelihood scale ScoreLevelMeaning 1RareExceptional circumstances only 2UnlikelyCould occur at some time 3PossibleMight occur at some time 4LikelyWill probably occur 5Almost CertainExpected in most circumstances Impact scale ScoreLevelMeaning 1NegligibleNo material impact 2MinorMinor disruption, easily recovered 3ModerateBusiness disruption, recoverable 4MajorSignificant disruption, regulatory attention 5SevereCatastrophic, regulatory breach Risk score bands & financial exposure 1–2🟢 LowMonitor · review annually< $50,000 3🟡 MediumRemediate within 90 days$50K – $500K 4🟠 HighRemediate within 30 days> $500K 5🔴 CriticalImmediate action required> $5,000,000
